steps.title: Match the circle form
steps.content:
A circle equation in standard form is
$$
(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2
$$
The given equation has $144$ on the right side, so $r^2=144$.

steps.title: Use the radius value
steps.content: The number on the right side is the radius squared, not the radius itself. Since the radius is positive, $$ r=\sqrt{144}=12 $$ The answer is 12.
